title: Claude API Sticker Shock: $100 a Week #minimax
summary: Keith AI discusses the cost of using Claude API, highlighting that typical weekly usage can reach $100. The video addresses concerns about API pricing and the financial implications for users and developers.
writeup: A short overview of three cost tiers for LLM API access, prompted by Claude API bills running $10 to $20 per day and over $100 per week. Cheaper providers (MiniMax, Deep Seek, Kimmy) are positioned as the main alternative, with the core claim that they perform at 80 to 90% of premium model quality for most general use cases at a fraction of the price. Monthly subscription plans (ChatGPT Pro at $20/month, and similar plans from other providers) round out the options as heavily subsidized access.

key_points: Claude API costs cited in the video reached $10 to $20 per day, totaling over $100 per week, establishing the practical price pressure motivating the comparison.

Cheaper LLM providers MiniMax, Deep Seek, and Kimmy are identified as the main cost-reduction path, delivering 80 to 90% of premium model performance for general-purpose tasks (the presenter excludes complex coding as a case where premium models retain a meaningful edge).
Monthly subscription plans, specifically ChatGPT Pro at $20 per month and equivalent plans from other providers, are described as heavily subsidized relative to pay-per-token API rates.
